Notice Of Motion And Motion To Contest The Good Faith Settlement Of Defendant, Cross-Defendant, And Cross-Complainant Kristin Tasia Kuhn
Off Calendar Sep-01-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Joseph Breall, Esq. 415-345-0545 of Breall & Breall for Plaintiff.
Marilou Bustonera, Esq. 925-446-3113 of Gilsleider McMahon Molinelli & Phan for Defendant Daniel Lormon.
Grace Harriet, Esq. 415-474-1900 of Kern Segal & Murphy for Defendant Justin Marsh.
Samuel Leff, Esq. 510-457-3440 of Hartsuyker Stratman Schwartz & Williams-Abrego for Defendant Kristin Kuhn.

RULING - A R G U E D.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT DANIEL LORMON's Motion To Contest The Good Faith Settlement Of Defendant, Cross-Defendant, And Cross-Complainant Kristin Tasia Kuhn.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Kristin Kuhn's application for determination of good faith settlement in this multi-vehicle fender-bender is granted. (CCP 877.6.) Daniel Lorman's motion to contest the Kuhn settlement is denied.

Kuhn's proposed settlement is $5,000. Lorman concedes plaintiff's past economic damages are "less than $3,800." The accident was in 2017 and suit was filed in 2018, but Lorman says it is too "early on" for any settlements. However, only a "rough approximation" of the settlor's liability is needed. (Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Associates (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488. 499.) This is particularly apt in a case like ours, where attorney fees and costs could soon outstrip damages - if they haven't already.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
